{/*
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
@catalog-layout: grouped-tables
@diagram: pipeline-flow
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
LAYOUT — grouped-tables is the right choice for scripts because:
  • Scale: 200+ scripts — per-item accordions are unusable at this count.
  • Scan pattern: engineers look up a script by name or filter by type,
    not by expanding individual items for detail.
  • Grouping: by type (audit, automation, generator, validator,
    remediator, dispatch) gives the most useful discovery path.

DIAGRAM — pipeline-flow renders a Mermaid flowchart from the
  normalised pipeline tier data in the registry. Generators MUST
  normalise raw pipeline strings to these canonical tiers:
    P1   — commit gate (pre-commit hook, hard-blocking)
    P2   — push gate  (pre-push hook)
    P3   — PR gate    (CI, Track B)
    P5   — scheduled  (post-merge cron, nightly)
    P6   — scheduled  (weekly / low-frequency cron)
    manual — on-demand developer tool
    indirect — library / utility module (not invoked directly)

━━ PIPELINE DIAGRAM ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Placed BEFORE the grouped tables. Shows which scripts fire at each
pipeline stage. Generator builds subgraph node labels from the
registry — one node per script name, truncated to 32 chars.

```mermaid
flowchart LR
  DEV([developer])

  subgraph P1[" 🔴 P1 — Commit Gate"]
    direction TB
    p1a["[script-name]"]
    p1b["[script-name]"]
  end

  subgraph P2[" 🟠 P2 — Push Gate"]
    direction TB
    p2a["[script-name]"]
  end

  subgraph P3[" 🟡 P3 — PR Gate"]
    direction TB
    p3a["[script-name]"]
    p3b["[script-name]"]
  end

  subgraph CRON[" 🔵 P5 / P6 — Scheduled"]
    direction TB
    cr1["[script-name]"]
  end

  subgraph MANUAL[" ⚪ Manual / On-demand"]
    direction TB
    m1["[script-name]"]
    m2["[script-name]"]
  end

  DEV -->|commit| P1
  P1  -->|push|   P2
  P2  -->|open PR| P3
  P3  -->|merge|  CRON
  DEV -.->|on-demand| MANUAL
```

━━ STATUS KEY ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Place after diagram, before grouped tables:
**Type key:** 🔍 audit · ⚙️ automation · 🏭 generator · ✅ validator · 🔧 remediator · 🚦 dispatch

━━ GROUPED TABLES (one ## per type) ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Columns: Script | Description | Pipeline | Concern | Usage
Script column: bare filename, not full path.
Pipeline column: normalised tier label (P1, P3, manual, indirect — NOT the raw JSDoc string).
Type order: audit → automation → generator → validator → remediator → dispatch

## 🔍 Audits

| Script | Description | Pipeline | Concern | Usage |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `[script-name.js]` | [description] | P3 | [concern] | `node [path] [--flags]` |

## ⚙️ Automations

| Script | Description | Pipeline | Concern | Usage |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `[script-name.js]` | [description] | manual | [concern] | `node [path] [--flags]` |

## 🏭 Generators

| Script | Description | Pipeline | Concern | Usage |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `[script-name.js]` | [description] | P1 | [concern] | `node [path] [--flags]` |

## ✅ Validators

| Script | Description | Pipeline | Concern | Usage |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `[script-name.js]` | [description] | P1, P3 | [concern] | `node [path] [--flags]` |

## 🔧 Remediators

| Script | Description | Pipeline | Concern | Usage |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `[script-name.js]` | [description] | manual | [concern] | `node [path] [--flags]` |

## 🚦 Dispatch

| Script | Description | Pipeline | Concern | Usage |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `[script-name.js]` | [description] | P1 | [concern] | `node [path] [--flags]` |

━━ INDIRECT / LIBRARY MODULES ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Library modules are not invoked directly — they are imported by other
scripts. List them in a collapsed accordion so they don't pollute the
main type-groups but are still discoverable.

<Accordion title="📚 Library Modules — [N]">

| Module | Description | Used by |
|---|---|---|
| `[module-name.js]` | [description] | [consumer script(s)] |

</Accordion>

━━ AUDIT SECTION (always at bottom when non-empty) ━━━━━━━━━━
Scripts with no `type` in their JSDoc header. These need attention.
Use a single <Accordion> — NOT wrapped in AccordionGroup.

<Accordion title="⚠️ Unclassified — [N scripts need @type]">

| Script | Path | Pipeline | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `[script-name.js]` | `[path]` | [raw pipeline value] | Missing @type in JSDoc header |

</Accordion>

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
*/}
